Document Lifecycle

Every purchase invoice in Merlin moves through a defined set of statuses. The lifecycle is enforced by the DocumentService, which only permits the transitions listed here. Understanding the lifecycle helps you understand which buttons are available at each stage and which permissions are required to use them.

Statuses

StatusMeaningTerminal?
receivedInvoice has been uploaded and processed; awaiting reviewNo
reviewedA staff member has checked the extracted dataNo
approvedInvoice has been authorised for postingNo
postedInvoice has been posted to the general ledgerYes
disputedInvoice has been flagged as disputed; returned to review queueNo
rejectedInvoice has been permanently rejectedYes

Terminal statuses (posted and rejected) cannot transition to any other status. Posted invoices cannot be edited or deleted.

The state machine

Valid transitions for purchase invoices:

FromCan transition to
receivedreviewed, approved, posted, disputed, rejected
reviewedapproved, posted, disputed
approvedposted, disputed
disputedreviewed, approved, posted, rejected
posted(terminal — no transitions)
rejected(terminal — no transitions)

Any attempt to make an invalid transition raises an InvalidDocumentStateException.

Transitions

Each transition maps to a method on DocumentService and requires a specific workflow permission.

Mark as reviewed

Calls DocumentService::markAsReviewed(). Allowed from: received. Records that a staff member has checked the AI-extracted data. The invoice does not have to be perfect to be marked reviewed — it simply signals that a human has looked at it. Requires the can-review-invoices permission.

Approve

Calls DocumentService::approve(). Allowed from: received, reviewed, disputed. Authorises the invoice for posting. This is a separate step from review, intended for organisations that have a two-person sign-off requirement. Requires the can-authorise-invoices permission.

Post

Calls DocumentService::post(). Allowed from: received, reviewed, approved, disputed. Posts the invoice to the general ledger. This is a terminal action — the invoice status becomes posted and no further changes are permitted. Requires the can-post-invoices permission.

Dispute

Calls DocumentService::dispute(). Allowed from: received, reviewed, approved. Marks the invoice as disputed — for example, because the amounts are incorrect or the invoice is queried with the supplier. A disputed invoice returns to the active review queue. NEEDS CONFIRMATION: which permission governs the dispute action.

Reject

Calls DocumentService::reject(). Allowed from: received, disputed. Permanently rejects the invoice. Use this for duplicate invoices, fraudulent invoices, or invoices that should never be posted. This is a terminal action. NEEDS CONFIRMATION: which permission governs the reject action.

Document activity log

Every transition is recorded in the document_activities table using the DocumentActivity model and the Spatie activity log package. Each log entry records: the document, the user who performed the action (the causer), the event name, and the timestamp. This log is read-only and cannot be edited or deleted. To view the activity history for an invoice, open the invoice detail panel — the activity log is shown at the bottom of the panel.

Permissions

ActionPermission required
Mark as reviewedcan-review-invoices
Approvecan-authorise-invoices
Postcan-post-invoices
DisputeNEEDS CONFIRMATION
RejectNEEDS CONFIRMATION
View activity logdocument-activities-view-any
